name: Sage and Apple Dressing
recipeIngredient: 1-1/2 tsp sage, rubbed from dried leaves
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat oven to 350 degrees F.
2. Spread bread cubes on a baking sheet and toast for 15 or 20 minutes, until lightly toasted.
3. Heat a large non-stick skillet over medium heat. Put celery and onion into the skillet, cover, and allow to sweat for 5 minutes.
4. Add two tablespoons of vegetable stock and stir to release any browned bits. Simmer until liquid evaporates.
5. Add apples and two more tablespoons of vegetable stock, simmer, stirring, until liquid evaporates.
6. Stir in sage, thyme, and 1/4 cup of vegetable stock, cover, reduce heat to low, and simmer until apples are tender (5 or 6 minutes).
7. Pour bread mixture into a large bowl, mix in cranberries, drizzle with remaining vegetable stock, and season with salt and pepper.
8. Transfer to a 10-cup casserole, lightly coated with non-stick spray, cover, and bake in a 325 degrees F oven for 35 to 45 minutes.
Note:
Apples which maintain their shape when baked are best; Northern Spy is the quintessential choice, but Golden Delicious would be good too.
